title: IOC Launches 2027 PhD Students and Early Career Academics Research Grant Programme for Olympic Studies Researchers Worldwide
summary: The International Olympic Committee (IOC), through its renowned Olympic Studies Centre (OSC), has officially launched the 2027 edition of the PhD Students and Early Career Academics Research Grant Programme. The initiative is designed to support scholars conducting research related to the Olympic Movement, Olympism, the Olympic Games, athletes, sports history, and the wider social and cultural impact of the Olympics across the world. The programme continues the IOC’s long-standing commitment to advancing academic research and encouraging global scholarly engagement in Olympic studies. Since its establishment in 1999, the grant programme has supported more than 148 researchers from different countries, helping them pursue innovative academic projects while also providing opportunities to access the IOC’s unique collections and historical archives in Lausanne, Switzerland. For researchers interested in sports history, sociology, international relations, culture, politics, media, education, and other areas linked to Olympism and the Olympic Games, this programme presents a major opportunity to receive institutional recognition and financial support for their academic work. About the IOC Olympic Studies Centre Research Grant Programme The IOC Olympic Studies Centre serves as the world’s leading hub for Olympic knowledge and research resources. The centre was created to preserve, develop, and share information related to the Olympic Movement and its influence on society. Through the PhD Students and Early Career Academics Research Grant Programme, the IOC aims to: Encourage high-quality academic research on Olympism and the Olympic Movement Promote interdisciplinary studies related to sports and society Support young and emerging scholars globally Increase global understanding of Olympic values and their societal impact Facilitate access to the IOC’s historical archives and collections The grant programme is open to scholars whose research explores different dimensions of the Olympic Games and Olympic culture. Topics may include: Olympic history and heritage Athlete development and representation Sports governance and policy Olympic education and values Gender and diversity in sports Media and communication in the Olympic Movement Social, political, and economic impact of the Olympics Olympic legacy and sustainability International relations through sports diplomacy Cultural influence of the Olympic Games The programme particularly values research conducted from humanities and social sciences perspectives. Access to Unique Olympic Research Resources One of the major advantages of this programme is access to the IOC Olympic Studies Centre’s world-class research resources. Selected researchers may have the opportunity to visit Lausanne, Switzerland, where they can consult the IOC’s extensive collections, including primary sources and historical archives related to the Olympic Movement. Researchers will also gain access to the Olympic World Library (OWL), a specialized library catalogue dedicated entirely to Olympic knowledge. The library contains more than 42,000 official and academic publications, making it one of the largest Olympic-related academic collections in the world. The IOC believes that giving scholars access to these resources helps improve the quality and originality of academic research while preserving Olympic heritage for future generations. Who Can Apply for the 2027 Research Grant Programme? The programme is specifically targeted at postgraduate researchers and early career academics involved in Olympic-related studies. Eligible applicants include: 1. PhD Students Applicants must currently be enrolled in a PhD degree programme within the humanities or social sciences. Their research must include Olympism, the Olympic Movement, or the Olympic Games as at least one of the main areas of focus. 2. Early Career Academics Academic staff members and postdoctoral fellows are also eligible if they completed their doctorate or equivalent highest degree in or after 2024. In some academic fields, a master’s degree may also qualify as the highest equivalent degree. This broad eligibility framework allows emerging scholars from different disciplines and countries to participate. Application Process and Deadline Interested applicants are required to prepare and submit their application forms directly to the IOC Olympic Studies Centre . According to the official announcement, all applications and related correspondence must be submitted before: Deadline: Tuesday, 22 September 2026 Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official programme rules, guidelines, and application form available on the IOC Olympic Studies Centre website before submitting their applications. The IOC has also announced that two online information sessions will be organized for researchers intending to apply. These sessions are expected to provide guidance on eligibility, application requirements, research expectations, and the overall selection process. Grant Selection Committee Applications will be reviewed by a Grant Programme Selection Committee composed of internationally recognized academic experts involved in Olympic studies, alongside representatives from the IOC Olympic Studies Centre. The committee evaluates applications based on: Academic quality Originality of the research topic Relevance to Olympic studies Methodological strength Potential contribution to the field This rigorous review process ensures that the programme supports impactful and high-quality academic work. Additional Olympic Research Opportunities Beyond the PhD and Early Career Academics Grant Programme, the IOC also offers the Advanced Olympic Research Grant Programme. This separate programme targets established researchers, including: University professors Lecturers Senior research fellows Applicants for the advanced programme must hold academic or research appointments and have completed their doctorate. The IOC has confirmed that applications for the 2027–2029 edition of the Advanced Olympic Research Grant Programme will open in October 2026. Why This Opportunity Matters The IOC research grant programme has become one of the most respected opportunities for scholars studying sports and society. It not only provides funding support but also offers researchers global academic visibility and access to exclusive Olympic resources. For young academics seeking to establish themselves in sports studies, sociology, history, or cultural research, this programme could significantly strengthen their academic profile and international research networks. Researchers interested in applying are encouraged to begin preparing their proposals early and participate in the upcoming online information sessions to better understand the application process. For more information, applicants can visit the IOC Olympic Studies Centre website or contact the centre directly via email at studies.centre@olympic.org . Opportunity Category Research Grant Eligible People and Regions Who Qualifies? Current PhD students Early career academics Academic staff members Postdoctoral fellows Researchers in humanities and social sciences Academic Qualifications Must be enrolled in a PhD programme OR Must have completed a doctorate or equivalent highest degree in or after 2024 Research must focus on Olympism, the Olympic Movement, or the Olympic Games Eligible Countries/Regions Open to applicants from all countries worldwide Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY here The post IOC Launches 2027 PhD Students and Early Career Academics Research Grant Programme for Olympic Studies Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Accelerate Africa Startup Programme: Empowering African Founders to Build Global Businesses
summary: African startups continue to transform industries, create jobs, and solve some of the continent’s biggest challenges through innovation and technology. However, many early-stage founders struggle with mentorship, funding access, strategic guidance, and global market expansion. To bridge this gap, Accelerate Africa has emerged as one of the continent’s most promising startup accelerator platforms focused on helping visionary African entrepreneurs build scalable and globally competitive businesses. Accelerate Africa is a startup accelerator programme designed specifically for ambitious founders across Africa who are building innovative solutions capable of addressing major social, economic, and technological challenges. The programme provides founders with hands-on mentorship, strategic support, access to investors, and practical guidance from experienced operators who have built and scaled successful African startups. According to the official platform, Accelerate Africa’s mission is to accelerate Africa’s bold and visionary founders in the earliest stages of building global businesses that solve Africa’s biggest challenges. What is Accelerate Africa? Accelerate Africa is an intensive accelerator programme created to support early-stage African startups through mentorship, workshops, advisory services, and networking opportunities. Unlike many traditional startup accelerators, Accelerate Africa focuses heavily on personalized founder support rather than a one-size-fits-all programme structure. The accelerator selects a limited number of founders who receive direct coaching and guidance from experienced entrepreneurs, operators, investors, and advisors with deep experience across African startup ecosystems. The programme was founded with the vision of helping African entrepreneurs create world-class businesses capable of competing globally while solving pressing continental problems. Accelerate Africa has already supported startups and founders linked to some of Africa’s most successful companies, including: Andela Flutterwave Moove Daystar Power DukaConnect Many of these businesses have grown into globally recognized companies, raised millions of dollars in funding, or been acquired by international corporations. Key Benefits of the Accelerate Africa Programme The programme offers several valuable benefits to startup founders who are looking to scale their businesses and attract investors. 1. One-on-One Coaching and Mentorship Selected founders receive personalized coaching from experienced startup operators, investors, and advisors who understand the realities of building businesses in Africa. This mentorship helps founders improve critical business areas such as: Business strategy Leadership Product development Market positioning Customer acquisition Scaling operations Investor readiness 2. Access to Investor Networks Accelerate Africa connects startups with local and international investors through demo days and investor showcases. Founders gain exposure to venture capital firms, angel investors, ecosystem builders, and strategic partners who may provide funding opportunities after the programme. According to programme information, startups may become eligible for optional post-programme investment opportunities ranging from $250,000 to $500,000 through Future Africa and partner networks after due diligence and approval processes. 3. Workshops Across Core Startup Areas The accelerator programme includes practical workshops and office hours covering: Storytelling and brand positioning Team building and leadership Product-market fit Go-to-market strategies Revenue growth Fundraising preparation Legal support Financial management These sessions are designed to help founders avoid common startup mistakes while improving their chances of long-term success. 4. Networking and Founder Community Participants also gain access to a strong community of ambitious African founders facing similar entrepreneurial challenges. The programme encourages peer learning, collaboration, and long-term networking opportunities across multiple African countries. 5. No Immediate Equity Requirement One of the unique features of Accelerate Africa is that the programme does not immediately take equity from startups upon admission. This founder-friendly structure allows entrepreneurs to access support and mentorship without giving away ownership early in the process. Programme Structure and Timeline Accelerate Africa typically runs cohort-based accelerator programmes involving a small group of selected startups. The programme generally includes: Virtual mentorship sessions In-person welcome week activities Workshops and advisory clinics Demo day preparation Investor pitch events Some programme editions run for approximately 8 to 12 weeks depending on the cohort structure. The accelerator prioritizes quality over quantity by selecting only a small number of founders for each cohort. Why Accelerate Africa Matters for African Startups Africa’s startup ecosystem continues to grow rapidly, but many founders still struggle with access to funding, mentorship, and growth support during the early stages of business development. Accelerate Africa aims to close this gap by helping entrepreneurs: Build scalable businesses Improve operational systems Reach international markets Raise venture capital funding Develop sustainable business models Create jobs and economic impact The programme’s focus on practical founder support and African market realities makes it especially valuable for entrepreneurs operating in emerging and underserved markets. Accelerate Africa has also positioned itself as a platform committed to supporting diversity and inclusion across the startup ecosystem, including women founders and entrepreneurs from different African countries and backgrounds. Accelerate Africa Portfolio and Impact According to official programme statistics, Accelerate Africa and its broader ecosystem have supported: 162 companies 170 founders More than $2.92 billion in funds raised Over 1,900 applications received These numbers reflect the growing influence of the accelerator within Africa’s startup and venture capital ecosystem. How to Apply Interested founders can apply directly through the official Accelerate Africa platform. Applicants are encouraged to prepare: A strong startup pitch Clear problem-solution explanation Business traction evidence Team information Growth strategy Market opportunity analysis Due to the highly competitive nature of the programme, founders are advised to apply early and ensure their applications clearly demonstrate scalability, innovation, and impact potential. For more details and applications, founders can visit: Accelerate Africa Official Website Opportunity Category Accelerator Programme / Grant Support Opportunity Eligible People and Regions Who Can Apply? Early-stage startup founders African entrepreneurs Tech-enabled startups Innovative business founders Startup co-founding teams Qualifications Must be building a scalable startup Must solve a meaningful African challenge Must demonstrate growth potential Startups should show innovation and market opportunity Some cohorts may prioritize startups with traction or MVPs Eligible Countries/Regions Open to founders across Africa African startups from multiple countries are encouraged to apply Discover more global accelerate programs on OFY here The post Accelerate Africa Startup Programme: Empowering African Founders to Build Global Businesses appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026: International Research Funding Opportunity for Early- and Mid-Career Researchers
summary: Applications are now open for the CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026, an exciting international research funding opportunity designed to support early- and mid-career researchers in building interdisciplinary and global research collaborations. Organised by Canadian Institute for Advanced Research , the Horizon Networks initiative aims to foster innovative research communities that can shape emerging fields of inquiry and establish the next generation of global thought leaders. The programme provides selected research teams with collaborative funding, professional development opportunities, international networking, and logistical support over a three-year period. Through Horizon Networks, researchers from diverse disciplines and institutions can work together to address some of the world’s most pressing challenges in education, childhood development, identity, technology, and civic engagement. About CIFAR Canadian Institute for Advanced Research , widely known as CIFAR, is a globally recognised research organisation headquartered in Canada. CIFAR brings together leading researchers from around the world to collaborate across disciplines and generate transformative ideas capable of solving complex global challenges. Supported by the governments of Canada, Alberta, and Québec, alongside international foundations, corporations, and partner organisations, CIFAR has built a strong reputation for advancing groundbreaking research and supporting academic innovation on a global scale. The organisation is particularly known for fostering interdisciplinary collaboration and creating environments where researchers can develop bold and innovative ideas that may not emerge within traditional academic structures. What Are Horizon Networks? Horizon Networks are collaborative international research networks led by early- to mid-career researchers. These networks are designed to: Build emerging interdisciplinary research communities Explore new fields of inquiry Strengthen international academic collaboration Develop future research leaders Encourage innovative thinking across disciplines Each selected Horizon Network will operate over a three-year term and receive support from CIFAR to pursue collaborative research activities. What the Programme Offers Selected Horizon Networks receive extensive support and funding, including: Three-Year Research Network Support Networks operate over a three-year period with long-term collaborative engagement among members. In-Person Meetings Network members meet twice per year in person, with all logistics, financial arrangements, and administrative coordination managed by CIFAR. Collaborative Research Funding Each Horizon Network receives funding distributed across the team to support collaborative research activities and network initiatives. Flexible Fund Management While CIFAR distributes the funding, Horizon Network leads are responsible for allocating and managing the funds among team members. Professional Development All network members participate in annual professional development opportunities designed to strengthen leadership, collaboration, and research impact. International and Interdisciplinary Collaboration Researchers gain access to international academic networks and interdisciplinary partnerships that can significantly enhance research visibility and long-term career growth. Eligibility Requirements The Horizon Networks programme specifically targets emerging research leaders and interdisciplinary collaboration. Leadership Requirements Each network must be co-led by researchers who are alumni of CIFAR’s early-career leadership programmes. Team Composition Each Horizon Network may include: Up to eight additional early- to mid-career faculty members Researchers from diverse institutions and disciplines Individuals who may not have previously participated in CIFAR programmes Institutional Diversity Network co-leaders are encouraged to recruit members from a broad range of institutions to ensure diversity of perspectives and expertise. Current Horizon Networks Streams for 2026 Applicants must submit proposals under one of the following thematic streams. Stream 1: Belonging with Nature, Technology and People in Early Childhood Supported by the Bezos Family Foundation, this stream explores how children’s relationships with nature, technology, and social environments shape development and belonging during early childhood. Stream 2: Adolescence – Belonging, Identity and Civic Engagement in a Changing World Also supported by the Bezos Family Foundation, this stream focuses on adolescent identity formation, civic participation, and social belonging in rapidly evolving societal contexts. Stream 3: Education and Learning Variability Between Ages 2–12 This stream is supported by the Jacobs CIFAR Research Fellowship programme and must be co-led by current or former Jacobs CIFAR Research Fellows. The stream investigates learning variability and educational development among children aged 2 to 12. Why This Opportunity Matters The Horizon Networks programme stands out because it empowers researchers at relatively early stages of their careers to: Lead international research collaborations Shape emerging academic fields Develop leadership and management skills Build influential research communities Access international visibility and funding opportunities Rather than supporting individual projects alone, the programme invests in building sustainable research ecosystems that can continue generating impact beyond the funding period. Review and Selection Process Applications undergo a multi-stage review process. Eligibility Screening CIFAR staff first review all submissions to ensure eligibility requirements are met. Reciprocal Peer Review Applicants also participate in a reciprocal peer review process, where proposed Horizon Network leads review and evaluate other applications. Final Decision Final funding decisions are made by CIFAR together with funding partners where applicable. Applicants submitting proposals agree to: Review assigned proposals Participate in peer evaluation Engage in the collaborative assessment process Applications are scored using a rubric aligned with the official selection criteria. Key Dates for CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026 Applications Open 6 May 2026 Application Deadline 17 June 2026 at 12 PM ET Peer Review Deadline 15 July 2026 Decision Notifications Summer 2026 Applicants are encouraged to begin building interdisciplinary teams and developing strong collaborative proposals early. Ideal Applicant Profile This opportunity is particularly suitable for: Early- and mid-career faculty researchers Interdisciplinary scholars Researchers interested in collaborative international work Academics exploring emerging research questions Scholars focused on childhood, education, technology, identity, civic engagement, and human development Strong proposals will typically demonstrate: Innovative interdisciplinary thinking Clear collaborative structures Leadership potential Strong research vision Global relevance and societal impact Benefits of Participating in Horizon Networks Researchers selected for Horizon Networks gain access to: International research collaborations Leadership development opportunities Funding flexibility Academic visibility Long-term interdisciplinary partnerships Exposure to high-level global research communities The programme also creates valuable opportunities for future grants, publications, policy influence, and large-scale research initiatives. Final Thoughts The CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026 programme offers a unique platform for emerging academic leaders to build influential interdisciplinary research collaborations on a global scale. Through funding, mentorship, networking, and professional development, the programme helps researchers shape new fields of inquiry while addressing important societal challenges. For early- and mid-career researchers seeking to establish themselves as international thought leaders, Horizon Networks represents a highly valuable and prestigious opportunity. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026: International Research Funding Opportunity for Early- and Mid-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded BIOFABRICATE PhD Studentship 2026 at Trinity College Dublin: Research Opportunity in Pharmaceutical Sciences and Sustainable Biomaterials
summary: Applications are now open for the fully funded BIOFABRICATE PhD Studentship 2026 at Trinity College Dublin , one of Europe’s most prestigious and internationally recognised universities. This exciting doctoral research opportunity is hosted within the School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences at Trinity College Dublin and is funded by the Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine (DAFM) in Ireland. The PhD studentship forms part of the larger BIOFABRICATE programme, a groundbreaking multi-institutional research initiative focused on developing sustainable bio-based materials and technologies from Irish forest biomass for applications across pharmaceuticals, construction, manufacturing, and packaging industries. This opportunity is ideal for candidates interested in pharmaceutical sciences, biomaterials, chemistry, sustainable manufacturing, and advanced pharmaceutical formulation research. About Trinity College Dublin Founded in 1592, Trinity College Dublin is Ireland’s leading university and one of the oldest and most respected institutions in Europe. Ranked 75th globally in the QS World University Rankings 2026, Trinity has built a strong international reputation for excellence in education, research, innovation, and global impact. The university is known for: World-class research facilities Strong international academic collaborations Leadership in sustainability and innovation Inclusive and diverse academic environment Commitment to equality, diversity, and work-life balance Trinity College Dublin also holds the Athena Swan Silver Award and is recognised as an EU Sustainable Gender Equality Champion. About the BIOFABRICATE Research Programme BIOFABRICATE stands for: Integrated Bio-based Innovations for Sustainable Manufacturing, Construction, and Pharmaceutical Applications from Irish Forest Biomass The programme is led by the University of Galway and involves collaboration with several Irish institutions, including: Trinity College Dublin Technological University Dublin Atlantic Technological University Dublin City University Teagasc Multiple industry partners The programme focuses on transforming Ireland’s growing forest biomass resources into advanced sustainable materials that can be applied across various industrial sectors. PhD Research Project Overview The selected PhD candidate will work under the supervision of Professor Anne Marie Healy in the School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences, with co-supervision from Dr. Ramesh Babu in the School of Chemistry. The project title is: “Evaluation of the Use of Microcrystalline Cellulose Generated from Irish Forestry Biomass in Pharmaceutical Solid Dosage Forms” The research will investigate the properties and pharmaceutical performance of microcrystalline cellulose (MCC) produced from Irish forest biomass (IFB-MCC). The project aims to: Evaluate the physical and chemical properties of IFB-MCC Compare IFB-MCC with commercially available pharmaceutical excipients Assess suitability for tablet direct compression applications Explore sustainable alternatives for pharmaceutical manufacturing Support innovation in environmentally friendly pharmaceutical materials This interdisciplinary project combines pharmaceutical sciences, biomaterials research, chemistry, and sustainability. What the Studentship Offers The fully funded PhD studentship provides candidates with: Full doctoral research funding Opportunity to work within a major national research programme Access to advanced laboratories and research infrastructure Collaboration with leading researchers and institutions Exposure to interdisciplinary and industry-linked research Academic mentorship from internationally recognised supervisors Opportunity to contribute to sustainable pharmaceutical innovation The position also offers valuable research and professional development opportunities within a collaborative academic environment. Ideal Candidate Profile The programme is suitable for highly motivated applicants with backgrounds in: Pharmaceutical Sciences Pharmacy Chemistry Materials Science Biomedical Sciences Chemical Engineering Pharmaceutical Technology Related scientific disciplines Candidates with research experience in pharmaceutical formulations, biomaterials, analytical chemistry, or sustainable materials may have an advantage. Application Requirements Applicants are required to submit the following documents via email to Professor Anne Marie Healy: Required Documents Full Curriculum Vitae (CV) A short motivation statement (maximum one page) outlining: Interest in the project Understanding of the research topic Academic and research motivations A writing sample such as: Published paper Thesis excerpt Academic report Contact details of two referees, including email addresses Application Deadline The application deadline is: 25 May 2026 at 12 noon (Irish time) Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews in June 2026. Trinity College Dublin’s Commitment to Inclusion Trinity College Dublin strongly values equality, diversity, and inclusion and welcomes applications from: International students Applicants with disabilities Individuals from non-traditional academic backgrounds Researchers displaced due to war or conflict Applicants with career breaks or caring responsibilities The university is committed to fostering an inclusive and supportive research environment where all scholars can thrive. Why This PhD Opportunity Stands Out This fully funded PhD studentship is particularly attractive because it combines: High-impact sustainability research Pharmaceutical innovation Interdisciplinary collaboration Industry relevance International academic exposure Access to leading Irish research institutions The project also aligns with growing global priorities surrounding sustainable manufacturing and environmentally responsible pharmaceutical development. Career Opportunities After the PhD Graduates completing this research programme may pursue careers in: Pharmaceutical research and development Sustainable materials innovation Academia and scientific research Biotechnology industries Pharmaceutical manufacturing Regulatory and formulation sciences Biomaterials and green chemistry sectors The interdisciplinary nature of the project equips researchers with highly transferable technical and analytical skills. Final Thoughts The BIOFABRICATE PhD Studentship 2026 at Trinity College Dublin offers an outstanding opportunity for aspiring researchers passionate about pharmaceutical sciences, sustainability, and biomaterials innovation. With full funding, expert supervision, collaborative research networks, and the chance to contribute to sustainable pharmaceutical technologies, this doctoral programme represents an excellent pathway for candidates seeking impactful research careers. Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare strong application materials and apply before the deadline. title: Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship 2026 in the UK: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Mandela Rhodes Alumni
summary: The Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship (SJMS) 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for outstanding Mandela Rhodes Alumni who aspire to pursue doctoral studies in the United Kingdom. Created through a partnership between the Mandela Rhodes Foundation and the Leverhulme Trust , this prestigious scholarship is designed to support emerging African leaders in advancing their academic, professional, and leadership journeys through fully funded PhD studies. The scholarship is exclusively available to Mandela Rhodes Alumni and aims to amplify the long-term impact of the Mandela Rhodes network by empowering scholars with access to world-class research institutions, international academic networks, and advanced doctoral training opportunities in the UK. Each year, only two scholarships are awarded, making the programme highly competitive and prestigious. About the Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship The Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship was established in 2016 as a collaboration between the Mandela Rhodes Foundation and the Leverhulme Trust. Initially known as the Leverhulme Mandela Rhodes Doctoral Scholarships, the programme was renamed in March 2020 in honour of Shaun Johnson, the founding Executive Director of the Mandela Rhodes Foundation, following his unexpected passing. The scholarship reflects Shaun Johnson’s vision of transformative leadership, education, and social impact across Africa. It seeks to empower Mandela Rhodes Alumni who demonstrate exceptional leadership potential and a commitment to contributing meaningfully to their communities and fields of expertise. The scholarship combines academic advancement with leadership development, ensuring that scholars are equipped not only with research skills but also with the networks and influence necessary to drive change across the African continent and beyond. Scholarship Benefits The Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship offers comprehensive financial support for selected scholars pursuing PhD studies in the United Kingdom. Benefits include: Full funding for tuition fees A stipend to cover living expenses Opportunity to study at leading UK universities Access to globally recognised supervisors and research facilities Exposure to international academic and professional networks Integration into the Leverhulme and Mandela Rhodes alumni communities The programme is designed to remove financial barriers and allow scholars to focus fully on their doctoral research and leadership development. Purpose and Objectives of the Scholarship The Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship aims to strengthen both vocational and leadership capacity among Mandela Rhodes Alumni. Supporting Vocational Capacity The programme seeks to: Enable scholars to deepen expertise in their chosen disciplines through doctoral study Provide access to world-class research environments and academic resources Connect scholars with leading supervisors and facilities that may not be available in their home countries Strengthening Leadership Capacity The scholarship also focuses on leadership development by: Supporting alumni who have clearly articulated the next phase of their leadership journey Encouraging scholars to use doctoral studies as a platform for long-term social and professional impact Expanding access to international academic and vocational influence networks through UK universities This unique combination of academic excellence and leadership development makes the SJMS one of the most impactful doctoral scholarship opportunities available to African scholars. Testimonial from a Previous Scholar According to Axolile Qina , a previous recipient of the scholarship: “The Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship has been an incredible opportunity to further my career aspirations in the academy. [ ] The support from Leverhulme has been exceptional. They ensured that we were firmly settled in upon our arrival, and organised an event where we could meet other Leverhulme researchers and fellow SJMS Scholars currently in the UK.” This highlights the strong support network and community experience offered through the programme. Eligibility Requirements The Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship is strictly open to Mandela Rhodes Alumni. Applicants must: Be alumni of the Mandela Rhodes Scholarship programme Intend to pursue doctoral studies in the United Kingdom Demonstrate strong academic and leadership potential Clearly articulate how the PhD aligns with their leadership and professional goals The programme is specifically designed for individuals who are committed to creating impact within their disciplines and communities. Application Process Applications for the Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship 2026 open on: 17 April 2026 The application deadline is: 17 May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials early due to the highly competitive nature of the programme. Application Steps Applicants should follow these steps: Check eligibility requirements Review the selection criteria carefully Understand the application process and timeline Prepare all required application documents Request application forms via email What Makes This Scholarship Unique? Unlike many traditional doctoral funding programmes, the Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship places equal importance on leadership development and academic excellence. Recipients become part of a dynamic network of scholars, researchers, and leaders committed to driving positive change across Africa and globally. The programme supports scholars not only financially but also through mentorship, networking, and community engagement opportunities. With only two scholarships awarded annually, recipients join a highly distinguished cohort of leaders and researchers. Why Pursue a PhD in the UK? The United Kingdom remains one of the world’s leading destinations for doctoral research due to: Globally recognised universities Advanced research infrastructure Diverse international academic communities Access to leading experts and supervisors Opportunities for international collaboration Strong research funding and innovation ecosystems Through the Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship, Mandela Rhodes Alumni can leverage these opportunities to further their academic and leadership ambitions. Final Thoughts The Shaun Johnson Memorial Scholarship 2026 offers Mandela Rhodes Alumni a transformative opportunity to pursue fully funded doctoral studies in the United Kingdom while strengthening their leadership impact and professional expertise. For scholars committed to academic excellence, social change, and leadership development, this programme provides a powerful platform to expand influence, deepen knowledge, and contribute meaningfully to Africa’s future. Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to begin preparations early and request the application forms before the deadline. title: Ferguson Scholarship 2026 at Aston University: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity for Students from Africa and India
summary: The Ferguson Scholarship 2026 at Aston University offers an exceptional opportunity for talented students from Africa and India to pursue postgraduate education in the United Kingdom with significant financial support. Supported by the Allan and Nesta Ferguson Charitable Trust, this prestigious scholarship programme is designed to improve access to higher education for students who may not otherwise have the financial means to study abroad. For the 2026/27 academic year, Aston University will award three Ferguson Scholarships to outstanding international students enrolling in selected postgraduate taught programmes in the Health and Life Sciences field. Each successful applicant will receive full tuition fee coverage worth £22,500 in addition to a £6,000 contribution towards living expenses. This scholarship represents more than just financial support. It is an opportunity to study at one of the United Kingdom’s most innovative universities, gain world-class academic training, access cutting-edge research opportunities, and build an international network that can shape future careers in science, healthcare, biotechnology, psychology, and public health. About Aston University Aston University is one of the UK’s leading universities known for academic excellence, employability, social mobility, and impactful research. Located in the heart of Birmingham, the university hosts a diverse international student community representing more than 120 countries worldwide. The university has earned several recognitions, including: University of the Year for Student Success (Daily Mail University Guide 2025) Ranked among the top universities in the UK by The Guardian University Guide 2025 Recognised for strong graduate employability and social mobility outcomes A respected institution in health sciences, biotechnology, psychology, engineering, and innovation Students at Aston University benefit from modern facilities, supportive academics, practical learning approaches, and strong industry connections that help graduates transition successfully into global careers. What the Ferguson Scholarship Covers The Ferguson Scholarship 2026 provides substantial financial support to selected students. The scholarship package includes: Full tuition fee coverage valued at £22,500 Additional £6,000 contribution toward living expenses Opportunity to study in the UK at a highly ranked institution Access to advanced laboratories, research facilities, and academic resources Exposure to international academic and professional networks This funding support significantly reduces the financial burden associated with studying abroad and allows students to focus on academic and professional development. Eligible Master’s Programmes Applicants must apply for one of the eligible postgraduate taught programmes at Aston University to qualify for the Ferguson Scholarship. Eligible programmes include: MSc Pharmaceutical Sciences MSc Drug Delivery MSc Pharmacokinetics MSc Stem Cells Regenerative Medicine MSc Biotechnology MSc Psychology (Conversion Programme) MSc Neuroscience for Drug Discovery MSc Addiction and Mental Health MSc Bioinformatics and Genomic Medicine Master of Public Health (MPH) These programmes are designed to equip students with specialised knowledge and practical skills in rapidly growing sectors of health and life sciences. Ferguson Scholarship Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the Ferguson Scholarship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: Academic Requirements Applicants must hold at least a 2:1 degree or an equivalent qualification in a relevant field related to the programme they are applying for. Admission Offer Candidates must first obtain either a conditional or unconditional offer from Aston University for one of the eligible master’s programmes. Nationality Requirements The scholarship is open exclusively to: Students from African countries Students from India Funding Status Applicants must be self-funded students. Sponsored students are not eligible for this scholarship. Agency Restriction Candidates referred through agencies will not be considered. Applications submitted through educational agents are automatically removed from review. Financial Evidence Successful candidates must provide evidence to UK Visas and Immigration (UKVI) demonstrating they can sufficiently contribute to their living costs while studying in the UK. How to Apply for the Ferguson Scholarship 2026 The application process involves two important stages. Step 1: Apply for an Eligible Course Applicants must first submit an application for one of the approved postgraduate programmes through Aston University’s official course application portal. Step 2: Complete the Scholarship Webform After submitting the course application, candidates must complete the Ferguson Scholarship application webform before the deadline. Applicants will need to provide their 9-digit Aston University application number beginning with “25”. Ferguson Scholarship Deadline 2026 The scholarship application portal will open on Monday, 2 March 2026. The final application deadline is: 29 May 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early to avoid technical issues or delays in processing admission applications. Selection and Results Announcement Successful candidates will be notified via email in June 2026 following the review process. The selection process is competitive and based on: Academic excellence Relevance of educational background Strength of application Potential contribution to the field of study Eligibility compliance Why You Should Apply The Ferguson Scholarship is an excellent opportunity for ambitious students seeking high-quality education in the UK without the overwhelming financial burden. Beyond the financial benefits, recipients gain access to: International academic exposure Advanced scientific and healthcare training Global career opportunities Research and innovation environments Diverse multicultural experiences Students graduating from Aston University often proceed into successful careers across healthcare, biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, neuroscience, psychology, and public health sectors worldwide. Life at Aston University Located in Birmingham, one of the UK’s most vibrant student cities, Aston University provides students with a supportive and inclusive environment. The university offers: Award-winning accommodation Modern research and teaching facilities Student support services Career development opportunities International student support Campus-based social and academic activities Its central location also provides excellent access to internships, networking opportunities, healthcare institutions, and industry partnerships. Final Thoughts The Ferguson Scholarship 2026 at Aston University is a life-changing opportunity for students from Africa and India who aspire to pursue postgraduate education in the UK. With full tuition coverage and additional living support, this programme removes major financial barriers and empowers talented students to achieve academic and professional excellence. Interested applicants should begin preparing their admission applications and scholarship materials early to maximise their chances of selection. title: PIDE Summer Internship Program 2026 in Pakistan: Elite Research Opportunity for Students and Graduates in Economics, Policy, and Data Science
summary: The Pakistan Institute of Development Economics (PIDE) has officially announced applications for its highly competitive Summer Internship Program 2026, offering students and recent graduates an exceptional opportunity to gain direct exposure to policy research, economic analysis, and development planning in Pakistan. For ambitious students interested in economics, public policy, data science, statistics, and related disciplines, this internship offers much more than workplace exposure. It is a career-shaping experience designed to immerse participants in real-world policy research under the guidance of some of Pakistan’s leading economists and development experts. The eight-week internship program will take place in Islamabad from July to August 2026 and is structured to help interns bridge the gap between academic theory and practical policy-making. Why the PIDE Summer Internship 2026 Is Important In today’s rapidly evolving economic and policy environment, governments and institutions increasingly rely on data-driven decisions, evidence-based policy recommendations, and economic forecasting. PIDE has long played a major role in shaping Pakistan’s national policy discussions and development strategies. Interning at PIDE therefore gives participants a rare chance to: Work within Pakistan’s premier public policy think tank Participate in meaningful research projects Understand how economic research influences governance Gain professional mentorship from experienced economists Build networks within the development and policy sector Unlike many traditional internships that focus mainly on administrative tasks, the PIDE Summer Internship is research-intensive and intellectually demanding. About the Pakistan Institute of Development Economics (PIDE) The Pakistan Institute of Development Economics (PIDE), headquartered in Islamabad, is one of Pakistan’s most respected research institutions specializing in: Economics Public policy Development studies Public finance Governance research Data analysis For decades, PIDE has contributed significantly to national policy debates, academic research, and development planning in Pakistan. Students selected for this internship will gain exposure to high-level policy discussions and real institutional research practices. Program Structure and Internship Overview The PIDE Summer Internship Program 2026 is a full-time, on-site internship lasting eight weeks. Program Duration July to August 2026 8 weeks Non-extendable Internship Location Islamabad, Pakistan Internship Format Full-time On-site Placement Areas Selected interns may be assigned to: Research Centres Ongoing Development Projects Technical Wings Policy Research Teams Core Components of the Internship The internship revolves around three major pillars: 1. Research Activities Interns will actively support policy and economic research through: Literature reviews Data collection Data interpretation Research assistance Policy analysis support 2. Writing Responsibilities Participants will contribute to: Research summaries Policy write-ups Analytical briefs Independent research papers Each intern is expected to complete at least one independent research piece during the program. 3. Professional Engagement Interns will participate in: Workshops Policy seminars Institutional discussions Research presentations Collaborative learning activities This ensures interns become fully integrated into PIDE’s intellectual and professional environment. Major Benefits of the PIDE Summer Internship 2026 1. Practical Research Experience Interns gain direct exposure to live policy and economic research projects, helping them understand how academic concepts are applied in national development planning. 2. Expert Mentorship Participants work closely with experienced: Economists Researchers Policy analysts Development professionals This mentorship can significantly influence academic and career growth. 3. Networking Opportunities Interns will build professional relationships with: Researchers Government advisors Policy experts Development sector professionals These connections may create future career opportunities. 4. Skill Development Participants develop practical skills in: Data handling Research methodology Policy communication Analytical writing Professional collaboration 5. Certificate of Completion Interns who successfully complete the program receive a Certificate of Completion based on performance. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must belong to one of the following categories: Undergraduate Students Must have completed at least 6 semesters Postgraduate Students Must have completed at least 2 semesters Recent Graduates Degree must have been awarded within the last 12 months Eligible Academic Fields Students from the following disciplines are encouraged to apply: Economics Public Policy Public Finance Data Science Statistics Related disciplines Statement of Purpose (SOP) Requirements A major component of the application is the Statement of Purpose (SOP). The SOP must: Be no longer than 300 words Explain the applicant’s interest in policy research Highlight relevant skills and academic background Describe how the applicant plans to contribute to the internship A strong SOP can significantly improve an applicant’s chances of selection. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the PIDE Summer Internship Program 2026 is: 31st May 2026 Late applications are unlikely to be considered. How to Apply Applicants can apply online through the official internship portal. APPLY HERE Contact Information Email: policy@pide.org.pk Phone: 051-9248026 Final Thoughts The PIDE Summer Internship Program 2026 stands out as one of Pakistan’s most prestigious opportunities for students and graduates interested in economics, development research, and public policy. Through practical research exposure, expert mentorship, and professional networking, the internship equips participants with valuable skills that can shape future careers in academia, government, international development organizations, and policy institutions. With limited seats and merit-based selection, interested applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their applications early and submit compelling Statements of Purpose before the deadline. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here . title: UN Women International Consultant on Violence Against Women Survey in Albania 2026–2027 (Up to 50 Working Days)
summary: The United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women) has officially announced applications for the position of International Consultant on Violence Against Women (VAW) Survey in Tirana, Albania. This high-level international consultancy presents an exceptional opportunity for experienced gender experts, statisticians, researchers, and violence prevention specialists to contribute to one of Europe’s most important national gender data initiatives. The application deadline is 18 May 2026. The consultancy will support Albania’s upcoming national Violence Against Women prevalence survey scheduled for implementation between 2026 and 2027. The selected consultant will work closely with the Albanian National Institute of Statistics (INSTAT) and UN Women Albania to ensure the survey meets global methodological and ethical standards while aligning with European Union gender-based violence data requirements. About UN Women UN Women works globally to eliminate discrimination against women and girls, promote women’s empowerment, and achieve gender equality across all sectors of society. In Albania, UN Women is implementing its Strategic Note 2022–2026, focusing on ensuring women and girls fully enjoy their human rights and actively contribute to the country’s sustainable development and European Union integration process. This consultancy is part of broader initiatives funded by Sida and the European Union to strengthen gender equality systems, improve violence prevention mechanisms, and advance gender statistics in Albania. About the Violence Against Women Survey Albania was among the first countries to conduct a comprehensive national Violence Against Women prevalence survey in 2018 following the adoption of the UN Sustainable Development Goals. The next survey round for 2026–2027 will: Measure the prevalence of violence against women in Albania Produce internationally comparable gender-based violence data Support monitoring of SDG Indicator 5.2 Contribute to the European Institute for Gender Equality (EIGE) Gender Equality Index Support Albania’s EU accession reporting obligations Align with the EU Survey on Gender-Based Violence (EU-GBV) Follow WHO and UN statistical guidelines on violence against women surveys The survey will also incorporate updated legal frameworks following Albania’s January 2026 law on the Prevention and Protection from Violence Against Women and Domestic Violence. Consultancy Overview Position Title International Consultant on Violence Against Women Survey Location Tirana, Albania Contract Type International Consultant Contract Contract Duration Up to 50 working days between June 2026 and September 2027 Work Arrangement Home-based consultancy with up to four trips to Tirana, Albania Application Deadline 18 May 2026 Key Responsibilities The selected international consultant will provide technical leadership and methodological guidance throughout all phases of the survey process. 1. Technical and Methodological Leadership The consultant will: Guide overall survey implementation Ensure alignment with EU-GBV methodology Ensure compliance with Eurostat standards Support implementation according to WHO and UN ethical guidelines Provide methodological oversight across all survey stages 2. Survey Design and Methodology Responsibilities include: Reviewing sample strategies and survey design Supporting questionnaire adaptation and validation Reviewing fieldwork procedures and protocols Supporting development of respondent materials Reviewing enumerator manuals and survey tools 3. Training and Capacity Building The consultant will: Support development of ethical protocols Develop training materials for field teams Conduct Training of Trainers sessions for INSTAT staff Strengthen institutional capacity for GBV survey implementation 4. Stakeholder Engagement Tasks include: Supporting consultations with national stakeholders Assisting the National Reference Group on VAW Survey Ensuring survey objectives align with national priorities 5. Pilot Testing and Quality Assurance The consultant will: Guide pre-test and pilot survey implementation Review pilot findings Recommend methodological adjustments Ensure quality assurance during field implementation 6. Data Processing and Analysis The consultant will support: Data cleaning and validation Weighting procedures Eurostat quality compliance Preparation of final datasets Development of tabulation plans Trend analysis comparing 2018 and 2026 data 7. Report Writing and Dissemination Responsibilities include: Co-leading analytical report preparation Supporting stakeholder presentations Participating in dissemination events Finalizing reports and presentations Deliverables and Timeline The consultancy includes several major deliverables across the project lifecycle. Key Deliverables Inception report and detailed methodology Validated survey instruments and ethical protocols Stakeholder consultation inputs Training package and Training of Trainers delivery Pilot survey recommendations Data processing and quality assurance support Tabulation plan and draft analytical report Final survey report and dissemination presentation The consultancy timeline runs from June 2026 through September 2027. Required Qualifications Applicants must possess strong expertise in gender statistics, violence against women research, and international survey methodologies. Education Applicants should have: A Master’s degree in Social Sciences, Gender Studies, Development Studies, Human Rights, Sociology, or related disciplines OR A first-level university degree combined with additional qualifying experience Professional Experience Candidates must demonstrate: Minimum 10 years of progressively responsible experience in GBV/VAW, social inclusion, or human rights Minimum 7 years of experience in GBV/VAW research and statistical processes Experience leading at least two household surveys on violence against women Expertise in Eurostat, EIGE, and FRA methodologies Familiarity with WHO Multi-Country Study standards Experience delivering trainings and facilitation Strong knowledge of statistical software such as STATA and SPSS Language Requirement Fluency in English is mandatory Core Competencies UN Women seeks professionals with: Integrity and professionalism Strong communication skills Gender sensitivity and awareness Accountability and stakeholder engagement Inclusive collaboration skills Organizational and teamwork abilities Commitment to quality and deadlines Why This Opportunity Matters This consultancy is more than a research assignment. It contributes directly to: Strengthening evidence-based policy making Improving protections for women and girls Advancing EU-aligned gender statistics Supporting implementation of the Istanbul Convention Enhancing Albania’s national response to gender-based violence Professionals selected for this role will help shape one of the region’s most significant gender equality data initiatives. How to Apply Applicants should carefully demonstrate: Technical expertise in VAW/GBV surveys International survey leadership experience Statistical and analytical capabilities Experience with ethical research methodologies Application Deadline: 18 May 2026 Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the Asia Journalism Fellowship 2026. Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . title: Québec Merit Scholarship Program for Foreign Students 2027–2028: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in Canada
summary: Canada continues to stand out as one of the world’s leading destinations for international education and advanced research. Among the most prestigious opportunities available to global scholars is the Québec Merit Scholarship Program for Foreign Students 2027–2028, funded by Québec’s Ministère de l’Enseignement supérieur (MES) and administered by the Fonds québécois de la recherche sur la nature et les technologies (FQRNT). This highly competitive scholarship program is designed to attract outstanding international students and researchers to Québec universities by supporting cutting-edge research, academic collaboration, and professional development. The program provides substantial financial support for master’s studies, doctoral studies, postdoctoral fellowships, and short-term research projects. For ambitious international students looking to study in Canada with financial support, this scholarship presents a remarkable opportunity to advance academic and research careers at globally recognized institutions such as Université Laval and other Québec universities. What Is the Québec Merit Scholarship Program for Foreign Students? The Québec Merit Scholarship Program for Foreign Students is an initiative established by Québec’s Ministry of Higher Education to strengthen international research partnerships and encourage academic excellence in Québec universities. The scholarship program supports foreign students and researchers who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and strong research potential. The initiative aims to: Foster international collaboration in research and innovation Attract highly qualified foreign talent to Québec Support advanced academic training and scientific development Promote research excellence in Québec institutions Encourage global academic exchange and professional growth The scholarship is administered by the Fonds québécois de la recherche sur la nature et les technologies (FQRNT), one of Québec’s leading research funding organizations. Scholarship Categories and Funding Amounts The program offers four major scholarship categories, each tailored to different academic and professional levels. 1. VM – Master’s Research Scholarships The VM scholarship supports international students pursuing research-based master’s degrees in Québec universities. Funding Details Award Amount: CAD $20,000 per year Duration: Up to 2 years This category is ideal for students seeking financial support while conducting advanced research during their master’s studies. 2. V1 – Doctoral Research Scholarships The V1 scholarship is designed for international PhD candidates pursuing doctoral research in Québec. Funding Details Award Amount: CAD $25,000 per year Duration: Up to 4 years Doctoral candidates can use this funding to support tuition, research expenses, accommodation, and living costs throughout their studies. 3. V2 – Postdoctoral Fellowships The V2 category supports international researchers undertaking postdoctoral research projects in Québec. Funding Details Award Amount: CAD $35,000 Duration: Non-renewable This fellowship helps postdoctoral researchers collaborate with leading academics and research institutions in Canada. 4. V3 – Short-Term Research or Professional Development This category supports short-term academic or professional research stays. Funding Details Award Amount: CAD $3,000 per month Maximum Duration: 4 months The V3 scholarship is suitable for: Researchers Graduate students Professionals seeking specialized research exposure Individuals pursuing academic collaboration opportunities Why This Scholarship Is Important The Québec Merit Scholarship Program offers more than just financial support. It provides recipients with the opportunity to become part of an internationally respected academic and research ecosystem. Key Benefits of the Scholarship 1. Significant Financial Support Recipients receive generous funding that can substantially reduce the financial burden of studying and conducting research in Canada. 2. Access to World-Class Research Institutions Québec universities are internationally recognized for research excellence across multiple disciplines. 3. International Networking Opportunities Scholars can collaborate with researchers, professors, and institutions from around the world. 4. Enhanced Academic and Career Growth Participation in a prestigious scholarship program strengthens academic profiles and future career prospects. 5. Exposure to Canadian Research and Innovation Canada is globally known for innovation, research infrastructure, and multicultural academic environments. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must carefully review the official eligibility rules before applying. General Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must: Be an international student or foreign researcher Meet the academic requirements for the selected scholarship category Obtain the support of a professor from a Québec university Be preselected by a Québec university before proceeding to the final selection stage Demonstrate strong academic performance and research potential Important Requirement: Professor Supervision One of the most critical requirements is obtaining the support of a professor who agrees to supervise the applicant’s research project. Without a supervising professor, applicants cannot proceed with the preselection process. Applicants are encouraged to: Research potential supervisors early Contact faculty members professionally Present a strong research proposal Demonstrate alignment with the professor’s research interests The Preselection Process Explained The scholarship selection process begins with internal preselection at a Québec university. Only candidates who are preselected by a participating Québec university will be invited by FQRNT to participate in the final selection round. How the Process Works Applicant identifies a supervising professor Applicant prepares required documents Candidate submits application to the Québec university Internal university review and preselection occur Selected candidates advance to FQRNT’s final evaluation stage The internal selection results are expected to be announced in late September. Application Deadline Université Laval Preselection Deadline Applicants seeking preselection through Université Laval must submit their applications by: June 17, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparations early, especially when contacting supervisors and preparing research proposals. Documents Typically Required Although requirements may vary by institution, applicants commonly need: Academic transcripts Research proposal Curriculum Vitae (CV) Letters of recommendation Proof of academic achievements Supervisor confirmation/support letter Language proficiency documents where applicable Interested candidates can apply by sending CV + Publications via Email: lusorapply@gmail.com Tips for a Strong Scholarship Application 1. Start Early Finding a supervisor and preparing documents can take significant time. 2. Build a Strong Research Proposal Clearly explain: Research objectives Methodology Expected impact Relevance to Québec research priorities 3. Tailor Your Communication When contacting professors: Personalize emails Reference their research Explain why you want to work with them 4. Highlight Academic Excellence Demonstrate: Research experience Publications (if any) Awards and achievements Leadership activities 5. Follow Official Instructions Carefully Incomplete applications may be rejected during preselection. Why Study in Québec, Canada? Québec is one of Canada’s most vibrant academic and research hubs. International students benefit from: High-quality education Multicultural communities Safe living environment Advanced research facilities Strong international student support systems Opportunities for collaboration and innovation Cities like Québec City and Montréal offer exceptional academic experiences combined with rich cultural heritage. Final Thoughts The Québec Merit Scholarship Program for Foreign Students 2027–2028 is an exceptional opportunity for international students and researchers who aspire to study and conduct research in Canada. With generous funding, access to prestigious universities, and opportunities for international collaboration, this scholarship can significantly shape the future of emerging researchers and academic professionals. Applicants should begin preparations early, secure academic supervision, and ensure all application materials meet institutional and FQRNT requirements before the deadline. For highly motivated students and researchers worldwide, this scholarship could be the gateway to world-class education and research excellence in Québec. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Québec Merit Scholarship Program for Foreign Students 2027–2028: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Young Voices for Europe 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Shape the Future EU Youth Strategy in Lyon, France
summary: Young people across Europe now have a rare opportunity to directly influence the future of youth policies in the European Union through the “Young Voices for Europe” Youth Policy Dialogue with Commissioner Glenn Micallef. Organized under the European Youth Portal, this fully funded youth engagement initiative will bring together 30 young participants in Lyon, France, from 15 to 17 July 2026 to contribute to discussions that will shape the next EU Youth Strategy. This exciting opportunity is designed for young people who are passionate about creating meaningful change in society, improving youth participation, and ensuring that the voices of young Europeans are heard at the highest policy-making levels. The initiative is led by Commissioner Glenn Micallef, the European Commissioner responsible for Intergenerational Fairness, Youth, Culture, and Sport. Participants will engage in high-level discussions on some of the most pressing challenges affecting young people today and help propose ideas that could influence future European youth policies. Importantly, no prior policy experience is required, making this opportunity accessible to a broad range of young people from different educational, professional, and social backgrounds. Why the “Young Voices for Europe” Dialogue Matters The European Union is currently preparing its next Youth Strategy, and this dialogue aims to ensure that young people themselves contribute to shaping it. Rather than developing policies without youth input, the EU wants young people to actively participate in conversations about: Inclusion in youth participation Challenges facing young Europeans Policy reforms needed to support youth development Ways the EU can better respond to young people’s needs This initiative represents an important step toward strengthening democratic participation among young people and promoting inclusive governance across Europe. Key Discussion Topics Selected participants will engage in conversations around several important themes, including: 1. Making Youth Participation More Inclusive Participants will discuss how youth engagement can become more accessible and inclusive regardless of: Social background Economic status Ethnicity Disability Educational level Geographic location The discussions will focus on ensuring that every young person has a chance to participate in shaping policies that affect their future. 2. Addressing the Biggest Challenges Young People Face The dialogue will also explore critical issues affecting youth today, such as: Employment and career opportunities Mental health Education access Social inclusion Housing challenges Digital transformation Climate concerns Youth representation in governance Participants will have the opportunity to share personal experiences and propose practical solutions. 3. Building a Youth-Centered EU Strategy Another important objective of the event is to understand how the European Union can better meet the real needs of young people. Young participants will help policymakers identify: Existing gaps in youth support systems Areas where policies are ineffective New ideas for future youth-focused initiatives Strategies for stronger youth engagement across Europe Who Can Apply? To be eligible for the “Young Voices for Europe” Youth Policy Dialogue, applicants must meet the following requirements: Be between 18 and 30 years old Currently reside in geographical Europe Have a good command of English Be available to attend the in-person event in Lyon from 15 to 17 July 2026 Have never participated in a Youth Policy Dialogue before The organizers encourage applications from young people of diverse backgrounds and experiences. What Is Covered? Selected participants will receive full support to attend the event in Lyon, France. The opportunity includes: Fully Funded Benefits Travel expenses to Lyon Accommodation for 15 and 16 July 2026 Meals during the event Local transportation and transfers Participation in organized side activities Networking opportunities with other young leaders across Europe This financial support ensures that young people can participate regardless of their economic background. Experience Beyond Policy Discussions In addition to the policy dialogue sessions, participants will also get the chance to explore the beautiful city of Lyon through organized cultural and social activities. This creates an opportunity for: Cross-cultural exchange Networking Collaboration with peers from different countries Building long-term international connections The event is expected to create a vibrant and engaging environment where young people can learn from one another while contributing to meaningful policy discussions. How to Apply Applicants must complete the official EU Survey application form. The application process requires: Full name Nationality Occupation Contact details Responses to general questions about youth participation in policymaking A short written response (maximum 200 words) related to the selected discussion topic Applicants will also indicate their preferred discussion topic during the application process. CLICK HERE TO APPLY Important Requirement Before the Event Selected participants must attend at least one online preparatory meeting before the in-person dialogue in Lyon. This preparatory session will help participants: Understand the event objectives Prepare discussion contributions Familiarize themselves with key themes Connect with organizers and fellow participants Application Deadline The deadline to submit applications is: 13 May 2026 at 23:59 CET Late applications are unlikely to be considered, so interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Why You Should Apply This opportunity offers much more than a trip to France. It provides young people with a direct platform to: Influence European youth policies Share ideas with EU decision-makers Develop leadership and communication skills Gain international exposure Expand professional and social networks Contribute to shaping the future of Europe For many participants, this could become a life-changing experience that opens doors to future international opportunities in policy, advocacy, leadership, and youth development. Final Thoughts The “Young Voices for Europe” Youth Policy Dialogue is an outstanding opportunity for young people who want their voices heard and wish to actively contribute to shaping Europe’s future. With all major expenses covered, no prior policy experience required, and direct interaction with EU leadership, this initiative provides a unique platform for young Europeans to influence decisions that affect their generation. Young people who are passionate about inclusion, youth empowerment, and policymaking should strongly consider applying before the deadline. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here .
